engineIntelligence.h2: Fiat Bravo II Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Fiat Bravo II in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Fiat Bravo II 192A8000 engine - diesel 1.9L replacement UK
knownFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow causing rough idle and reduced power on high-mileage units.
DPF regeneration failure - frequent short journeys prevent complete burn-off leading to blockage and warning lights.

cards.altText: Fiat Bravo II 198A1000 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade after 80k miles causing rattling noise and potential chain skip.
Turbocharger actuator failure - wastegate sticks leading to loss of boost and reduced performance on T-Jet units.

cards.altText: Fiat Bravo II 192B2000 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough running common after 70k miles on naturally aspirated 1.4 Fire units.
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage examples leads to elevated oil usage between services.

engineGuide.h2: Fiat Bravo II Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Fiat Bravo II across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The entry-level 1.9 Multijet brought efficient diesel power to the Bravo II, popular with high-mileage drivers but prone to EGR issues.
compatibleVariants: Dynamic 1.9 Multijet, Emotion 1.9 Multijet, Sporting 1.9 Multijet
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts flow causing rough idle and warning lights after 70k miles
DPF regeneration failure - frequent short journeys prevent complete burn-off leading to blockage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192A8000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: The high-output 16-valve 1.9 Multijet delivered 150hp for Sporting variants, offering strong torque but requiring careful maintenance.
commonFailures: Turbocharger actuator wear - wastegate sticks causing loss of boost and reduced performance on high-mileage units
Injector coding drift - fuel injectors require recalibration after replacement to maintain smooth running
entries.cta: Get quotes for 937A5000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The refined 1.6 Multijet replaced the 1.9 in 2008, offering improved emissions and fuel economy for urban Bravo II drivers.
compatibleVariants: Active 1.6 Multijet, Dynamic 1.6 Multijet, Emotion 1.6 Multijet, Dynamic Eco 1.6 Multijet
commonFailures: EGR cooler leaks - coolant contamination of exhaust gases triggers warning lights and reduced power
DPF pressure sensor failure - false regeneration requests increase fuel consumption on stop-start driving
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192B3000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The flagship 2.0 Multijet brought 165hp to the Bravo II Sporting, delivering performance diesel capability for enthusiasts.
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - reduced rail pressure causes hard starting and power loss on high-mileage examples
Timing chain tensioner fatigue - plastic guides wear after 90k miles risking chain skip on interference engine
entries.cta: Get quotes for 844A2000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated 1.4 Fire provided reliable, economical transport for entry-level Bravo II Active and Dynamic variants.
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough idle common after 70k miles on naturally aspirated 1.4 Fire units
Oil consumption increase - piston ring wear on high-mileage examples leads to elevated oil usage between services
entries.cta: Get quotes for 192B2000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: The turbocharged 1.4 T-Jet brought spirited performance to Dynamic, Emotion and Sporting variants, though timing chain care is essential.
compatibleVariants: Dynamic 1.4 T-Jet, Emotion 1.4 T-Jet, Sporting 1.4 T-Jet, Cup 1.4 T-Jet
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade after 80k miles causing rattling noise and potential chain skip
Turbocharger actuator failure - wastegate sticks leading to loss of boost and reduced performance on T-Jet units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 198A1000 Fiat Bravo II engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Fiat Bravo II engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Fiat Bravo II Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Fiat Bravo II's Multijet diesel and T-Jet petrol engines are generally robust, but EGR/DPF issues on diesels and timing chain wear on T-Jet units dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Bravo II.

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Why Fiat Bravo II Multijet Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Bravo II Dynamic 1.6/1.9 Multijet, Emotion 1.9 Multijet, Sporting 1.9 Multijet (2007-2014, codes 192A8000, 937A5000, 192B3000)
problems.rootCause: Carbon deposits from exhaust gases accumulate in the EGR valve and cooler, restricting flow and triggering fault codes that limit engine performance.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - EGR Cleaning/Blanking - Remove and clean valve or fit blanking plate
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve removal, ultrasonic cleaning or blanking kit fit, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; may trigger MOT emissions failure if blanked

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - EGR Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned EGR valve
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New valve supply and fit, gasket replacement, diagnostic reset
repairOptions.longevity: 2-3 year solution if driving patterns remain unchanged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt Multijet unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with upgraded EGR components, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution addressing root cause; best value for high-mileage cars

problems.recommendation: If your Bravo II has under 80k miles and the EGR is the only fault, cleaning or replacement offers good value. For cars over 100k miles with additional wear signs, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components often proves more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Multijet engine prices for your Fiat Bravo II

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - Why Fiat Bravo II Diesels Show Warning Lights
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Bravo II Dynamic Eco 1.6/1.9 Multijet, Emotion 1.9 Multijet DPF, Sporting 1.9 Multijet DPF (2008-2014, codes 192A8000, 937A5000, 192B3000)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent the DPF reaching temperatures needed for automatic regeneration, causing soot buildup that triggers warning lights and limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Forced Regeneration - Dealer diagnostic tool initiates burn cycle
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD connection, extended high-rev drive cycle, fault code clear
repairOptions.longevity: Works if blockage is mild; requires motorway driving afterwards

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Removal & Clean - Off-car chemical or thermal cleaning
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: DPF removal, specialist cleaning process, refit and ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for moderate blockages; 18-24 month typical lifespan post-clean

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt Multijet with new DPF
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with new or cleaned DPF, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage cars or repeated DPF failures; eliminates recurring costs

problems.recommendation: For Bravo II owners doing mostly urban miles, a cleaned DPF combined with driving habit changes can extend life. If regeneration failures recur within 12 months, budget for a rebuilt engine to avoid repeated repair bills.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Bravo II DPF-compatible rebuilt engines

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Why Fiat Bravo II T-Jet Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Bravo II Dynamic 1.4 T-Jet, Emotion 1.4 T-Jet, Sporting 1.4 T-Jet, Cup 1.4 T-Jet (2007-2014, code 198A1000)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guide wear allows chain slack, causing rattling on cold start and risking chain skip on this interference engine.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Kit - Replace tensioner and guides before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front cover removal, new tensioner/guides/chain, seals, timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended at 70k miles; prevents catastrophic engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt T-Jet unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with upgraded timing components, bench testing, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if chain has skipped; best value for cars over 90k miles

problems.recommendation: If you hear cold-start rattling on your T-Jet Bravo II, act immediately - a preventative chain kit is far cheaper than repairing valve damage from a skipped chain. For cars already showing misfires or loss of compression, replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt T-Jet engine prices for your Fiat Bravo II

problems.h4: Turbocharger Actuator Failure - Why Fiat Bravo II Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Bravo II Sporting 1.4 T-Jet, Sporting 1.9/2.0 Multijet (2007-2014, codes 198A1000, 937A5000, 844A2000)
problems.rootCause: Turbo wastegate actuator rods seize or linkage wears, preventing boost control and triggering limp mode with reduced power output.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Actuator Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned turbo actuator
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator removal, new unit fit, calibration, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Works if turbo internals are sound; 2-3 year typical lifespan

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with reconditioned turbo, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage cars or repeated turbo issues; eliminates recurring costs

problems.recommendation: If your Bravo II shows boost loss but no smoke or unusual noises, actuator replacement may suffice. For cars with additional turbo wear signs (smoke, whine), a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Find Fiat Bravo II turbo-replacement engine deals

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Why Fiat Bravo II Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Fiat Bravo II all variants with 1.4 Fire, 1.4 T-Jet, 1.6/1.9/2.0 Multijet (2007-2014, all codes)
problems.rootCause: Coolant degradation, overheating episodes, or manufacturing variances cause gasket failure, allowing coolant/oil mixing and compression loss.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Gasket Replacement - Strip top end, replace gasket, machine head
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, gasket replacement, pressure test, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; requires thorough cooling system overhaul

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with upgraded gasket, cooling system check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage cars or if block shows scoring; faster turnaround

problems.recommendation: Head gasket repair is labour-intensive and risks missing underlying block damage. For Bravo II engines over 100k miles, a rebuilt unit with upgraded components often proves more reliable and cost-effective than a top-end repair.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Bravo II head-gasket-proof rebuilt engines

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Fiat Bravo II
finalCta.paragraph: The Fiat Bravo II's engines are capable of high mileage with proper care, but EGR/DPF issues on diesels and timing chain wear on T-Jet units are common failure points that can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Fiat Bravo II specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Fiat Bravo II Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Fiat Bravo II and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Fiat Bravo II is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Fiat Bravo II Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Fiat Bravo II 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3 Multijet was the entry diesel option for early Bravo II Active variants, offering excellent fuel economy but limited power for the car's weight, making it best suited to urban driving.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Bravo II 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Bravo II 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6 Multijet replaced the 1.3 and became the core diesel choice for Dynamic and Emotion variants, balancing torque, economy and emissions compliance for UK drivers.
commonFailurePoints: DPF pressure sensor failures on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Bravo II 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Fiat Bravo II 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9 Multijet powered most early Bravo II diesels, offering strong torque and proven reliability but requiring careful maintenance of EGR and DPF systems to avoid costly failures.

engineYears.h2: Fiat Bravo II Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Fiat Bravo II's 2007-2014 production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Bravo II launched with 1.4 Fire petrol and 1.9 Multijet diesel engines, establishing the core powertrain lineup for early UK models.
keyChanges: Bravo II replaces Stilo in UK lineup with new 198 chassis
1.4 Fire 90hp petrol and 1.9 Multijet 120/150hp diesel engines introduced
Active, Dynamic, Emotion and Sport trim levels launched
Dualogic semi-automatic transmission option added for select variants
knownFor: Strong initial UK sales driven by value proposition vs rivals
Early 1.9 Multijet units show EGR clogging from 60k miles
T-Jet timing chain rattling reported on cold starts after 70k miles
DPF-equipped variants require motorway runs to maintain regeneration

years.description: The 2010 facelift introduced refined 1.6 Multijet diesel engines and Euro 5 emissions compliance, improving economy and reducing failure rates on later models.
keyChanges: 1.6 Multijet 105/120hp replaces 1.9 Multijet in most variants
Euro 5 emissions compliance across range with updated DPF systems
Exterior refresh with new front/rear styling and interior trim updates
Eco Pack variants introduced with start-stop and low-rolling-resistance tyres
popularModels: Fiat Bravo II Dynamic 1.6 Multijet Eco
knownFor: Improved DPF reliability on 1.6 Multijet vs earlier 1.9 units
T-Jet timing chain issues persist but reduced with revised tensioners
2.0 Multijet flagship offers strong performance but specialist rebuild required
Eco Pack variants show better urban fuel economy but DPF care still essential

years.description: The final Bravo II models carried over the refined 1.6/2.0 Multijet and 1.4 T-Jet engines, with limited new stock making reconditioned units the primary replacement option.
keyChanges: Production winds down with focus on clearing remaining stock
No new engine introductions; carry-over of proven 1.6/2.0 Multijet and 1.4 T-Jet
Increased emphasis on reconditioned engine supply as new units become scarce
Final UK registrations peak in 2014 before model discontinuation
knownFor: Late-model units benefit from all previous reliability updates

faq.h2: Fiat Bravo II Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Fiat Bravo II engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Fiat Bravo II engine typically costs £1,800-£3,400 supply-only depending on engine code and variant, with 1.4 Fire petrol at the lower end and 2.0 Multijet diesel at the higher end of the range.
keyPoints: 1.4 Fire petrol rebuilt: £1,800-£2,200 supply only
1.4 T-Jet petrol rebuilt: £2,000-£2,600 supply only
1.6/1.9 Multijet diesel rebuilt: £2,200-£3,000 supply only
2.0 Multijet diesel rebuilt: £2,500-£3,400 supply only
Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on garage location
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Fiat Bravo II engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit the Fiat Bravo II?
items.answer: The Fiat Bravo II uses engine codes 192B2000 (1.4 Fire petrol), 198A1000 (1.4 T-Jet petrol), 192A8000/937A5000 (1.9 Multijet diesel), 192B3000 (1.6 Multijet) and 844A2000 (2.0 Multijet).
keyPoints: Enter your registration for instant engine code verification
192B2000 fits Active/Dynamic 1.4 petrol variants
198A1000 fits Dynamic/Emotion/Sporting 1.4 T-Jet variants
192A8000/937A5000 fit Dynamic/Emotion/Sporting 1.9 diesel variants
Always confirm code compatibility before ordering replacement
items.cta: Check your Fiat Bravo II engine code compatibility now

items.question: Is a reconditioned Fiat Bravo II engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes, reconditioned Fiat Bravo II engines from vetted UK specialists are reliable when rebuilt to OEM specifications with upgraded components addressing known failure points like EGR valves and timing chains.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include new gaskets, seals and bearings
Bench-tested before dispatch to ensure performance
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Upgraded components target common Bravo II failure patterns
items.cta: Find reconditioned Fiat Bravo II engines with warranty

items.question: How long does Fiat Bravo II engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround for a Fiat Bravo II engine replacement is 3-7 working days from order to collection, depending on engine code availability and garage scheduling for supply & fit.
keyPoints: Common codes like 192B2000 often available for next-day dispatch
Specialist codes like 844A2000 may require 5-7 day lead time
Supply & fit bookings depend on garage availability in your area
ECU coding and road test add 1-2 hours to fit appointment
items.cta: Get quotes for Fiat Bravo II engine supply & fit near you

items.question: Do I need ECU reprogramming for Fiat Bravo II engine swap?
items.answer: Yes, most Fiat Bravo II engine swaps require ECU adaptation or coding to match the replacement engine's immobiliser and ensure smooth running, especially for Multijet diesel and T-Jet petrol units.
keyPoints: Professional supply & fit includes ECU coding as standard
Immobiliser pairing prevents no-start issues post-installation
Diagnostic reset clears fault codes from previous engine
Always confirm coding is included in your quote
items.cta: Compare Fiat Bravo II engine quotes with ECU coding included

items.question: Will a replacement Fiat Bravo II engine pass MOT?
items.answer: Yes, rebuilt Fiat Bravo II engines supplied through EngineMarket are rebuilt to MOT-compliant standards with emissions systems intact, ensuring your vehicle remains road-legal post-replacement.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units retain original DPF/EGR hardware where fitted
Emissions testing included in bench test pre-dispatch
Documentation provided for MOT tester verification
12-month warranty covers MOT-related failures
items.cta: Find MOT-compliant Fiat Bravo II replacement engines

items.question: Can I fit a Fiat Bravo II engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible for experienced mechanics, DIY Fiat Bravo II engine fitting is not recommended due to ECU coding requirements, torque specifications and warranty implications.
keyPoints: ECU immobiliser pairing requires specialist diagnostic tools
Incorrect torque specs risk head gasket or bearing failure
Professional fit required to maintain 12-month warranty
Supply-only option available for qualified independent garages
items.cta: Find professional Fiat Bravo II engine fitting near you

items.question: What warranty comes with Fiat Bravo II replacement engines?
items.answer: Every Fiat Bravo II replacement engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for rebuild-related failures.
keyPoints: Warranty valid at any UK garage for claim convenience
Covers parts and labour for rebuild-related failures
Unlimited mileage removes usage restrictions for high-mileage drivers
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier pre-order
